name: Stuffed Escarole Leaves with Marinated Chickp
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chickpeas in a large bowl and cover with 9 cups water. Refrigerate and let soak overnight.
2. In a large pot, bring two quarts of water to a boil. Add sage and bay leaves.
3. Drain the chickpeas and rinse well. Add chickpeas to the boiling water and cook until soft, about 30 to 45 minutes. Drain the chickpeas and reserve 2 cups of cooking liquid. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, lightly mash, and set aside to cool.
4. In a large bowl, toss tomatoes with garlic and parsley. Add cooled chickpeas and enough reserved cooking liquid to moisten. Add vinegar to taste.
5. Boil a small saucepan of water and prepare an ice bath in a large bowl filled with ice water.
6. Place escarole leaves in a strainer and submerge in boiling water until soft, about 2 minutes. Remove from water and immediately plunge into the ice bath. Drain and pat dry.
7. Lay leaves out flat and place about 2 to 3 tablespoons of chickpea mixture at the broad end of each leaf. Roll up like a cigar, turning sides inward to enclose the filling.
8. Serve at room temperature.
